concerns.text: Task T012 implements a hard stop at N < 130. This silently relaxes the spec's FR-002 and Edge Cases which mandate a hard stop only at N < 30 and allow proceeding with warnings for 30 <= N < 100. The task enforces a stricter constraint than the spec without explicit authorization, violating the 'no silent constitution drift' principle.

Task T025 mandates an 'UNCONDITIONAL' robustness check, explicitly removing the 'r > 0.3' condition. This violates FR-006 and the User Story 3 acceptance criteria which require the check to be conditional on correlation > 0.3. The task drops a mandatory acceptance criterion from the spec.

Task T019a moves the construct validity check (distinct constructs verification) to the Foundational Phase, whereas the spec (FR-003) ties this verification to the regression modeling step (US-2). While the logic is sound, the task decouples the validation from the specific FR-003 context, potentially creating a semantic drift where the check is treated as a generic infrastructure item rather than a specific requirement of the regression model's validity.
